content: Just as English language learners deepen their writing mastery by iterative reflection on their own output to spot grammatical and structural gaps, LLMs can borrow structured reflective scaffolding from second-language writing pedagogy to refine their self-reflection mechanisms, turning vague self-critique into targeted improvement aligned with explicit performance standards. This framework reframes LLM self-reflection not as a specialized AI trick, but as a general learning process that has been refined in human education for decades.
